# Critical assessment

## Useful

The verified selectors, direct LinkedIn routes, canonical URL handling, activity checks, company/person discovery, and supervised comment flow can remove most manual research. The existing browser work was verified in German and already covers company search, people search, profile pages, company pages, posts, comments, jobs, and supervised writes.

## Overreach

“Search as many companies as we want” is the wrong operating principle. More records do not create visibility. Large runs increase duplicates, weak matches, stale profiles, platform friction, and review work. For one week, 30 relevant companies and 20 active people are more useful than 500 unqualified records.

Fully autonomous commenting is also a bad default. A technically correct selector does not make a comment socially appropriate. Context, tone, timing, repetition, and reputation matter. Therefore research and drafting may be automated, but each visible comment remains individually supervised.

## This week's operating thesis

Visibility will not come from publishing into an empty network. It will come from repeatedly contributing to existing conversations where the target audience already participates. The campaign therefore prioritizes:

1. Mapping active industrial people and accounts.
2. Finding recent posts with a real opening for expertise.
3. Posting a small number of brief, specific, human-approved comments.

The objective is not raw impressions. It is replies, repeated recognition, relevant profile visits, and qualified conversations.
